title: Methods for providing intermediates in the synthesis of atorvastatin.
abstract: The invention relates to the field of medicinal chemistry, In particular, it relates to methods for providing intermediates in the synthesis of Atorvastatin, a competitive inhibitor of HMG-Co A reductase. Provided is a process for providing a compound having a Formula (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt, ester, amide or stereoisomer thereof, comprising reacting in a 4 component Ugi-reaction in a single reaction mixture the compounds of formula A, formula B, formula C and formula D.
claims:
Claims Claims A process for providing a compound having a Formula (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt or stereoisomer thereof, comprising reacting in a single reaction mixture the compounds of formula A, formula B, formula C and formula D, wherein the compounds are defined as follows: i) Formula A ii) Formula B ,CHO iii) Formula C iv) Formula D wherein Rl is a linear or branched C1-C5 alkyl, C3-C8 aroyl or C3-C8 heteroaroyl, optionally substituted; R2 is benzyl, naphthyl or cyclohexyl, optionally substituted; or phenyl optionally substituted with fluorine, chlorine, bromine, hydroxy! or trifluoromethyl; pyridinyl or pyridinyl substituted with fluorine, chlorine, bromine, hydroxy! or trifluoromethyl; or alkyl of from one to seven carbon atoms; or wherein R2 is selected from the group consisting of I, Br, CI, trifluoromethanesulfonate, methanesulfonate, 4-methylphenylsulfonate, boronic acid, boronic acid esters, nitro, diazonium tetrachloroborate, diazonium tetrafluroborate, diethyltriazene, aryliodonium triflate, aryliodonium mesylate, aryliodonium tosylate, aryliodonium iodide, trimethylammonium chloride, trimethylammonium iodide and trimethylammonium bromide R3 is a branched C3-C8 alkyl, a carboxy protecting group selected from benzyl, diphenylmethyl, trityl, 4-nitrobenzyl, and 4-methoxybenzyl, preferably wherein R3 is -C(CH3)3; R4 and R5 are each independently C 1-C 10 alkyl, C(0)Ra, - SiRbRcRd, or R4 and R5 together form isopropyl; wherein Ra is H, C 1-C 10 alkyl, C3-C8 cycloalkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroaryl or heteroalkyl, optionally substituted with aryl, heteroaryl, C 1-C6 alkyl, halogen, and wherein Rb, Rc and Rd are each independently C1-C6 alkyl; 

 R6 is Ci-Cio alkyl, C3-C8 cycloalkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroaryl or heteroalkyl, optionally substituted with aryl, heteroaryl, Ci-Ce alkyl, d-Ce alkoxy, halogen, or CN, -SiRbRcRd wherein Rb, Rc and Rd are each independently Ci-Ce alkyl. 2. Process according to claim 1, wherein Rl is a linear or branched C1-C5 alkyl, optionally substituted with a halogen, 3. Process according to claim 2, wherein Rl is selected from isopropyl, ethyl, trifluoromethyl and difluoromethyl, preferably isopropyl. 4. Process according to any one of claims 1-3, wherein R2 is a substituted C5- or C6-membered aryl or heteroaryl 5. Process according to any one of claims 1-4, wherein R2 is a phenyl or substituted phenyl, preferably a phenyl substituted with a halogen, more preferably wherein R2 is para-fluorophenyl. 6. Process according to claim 5, wherein Rl is isopropyl and R2 is para-fluorophenyl. 7. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein R3 is a branched C3-C8 alkyl, preferably wherein R3 is -C(CH3)3 8. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein R4 and R5 are each independently C1-C10 alkyl, C(0)Ra, or -SiRbRcRd. 9. Process according to any one of claims 1-7, wherein R4 and R5 together form isopropyl. 10. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein R6 is selected from the group consisting of tert-butyl-, l, l-dimethyl-3,3- dimethyl-butyl-, benzyl-, p-methoxybenzyl-, diphenylmethyl-, di(p-methoxy- phenyl)methyl-, allyl-, 1-adamantyl-, 2-adamantyl-, 2- (dialkoxymethyl)phenyl-, trityl-, cyclohexene-l-yl-, 2-propane-2-methyl-3- olyl-, 2-cyanoethyl-, TMS-CN (TMS = trimethylsilyl), AgCN, 2-propane-2- methyl-3-alkyloxycarbonyloxyl-, or a polymer bound form thereof, and 4- nitr o - 2 -m ethoxyphenyl . 11. Process according to claim 10, wherein R6 is 2-nitro-4- methoxyphenyl. 12. Process according to claim 1, for providing (3R,5R)-7-(N- (carboxy(4-fluorophenyl)methyl)isobutyramido)-3,5-dihydroxyheptanoic acid. 13. Process according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising hydrolyzing the bisamide compound according to formula I under acid, alkaline or hydrogenolytic conditions to obtain a monoamide compound according to Formula II wherein R1-R5 are as defined in any one of claims 1-9, and wherein R7 is H, alkyl, aroyl, or heteroaroyl, preferably wherein R7 is methyl, ethyl, benzyl or H. 14. Process according to claim 13, further comprising cyclization of the monoamide compound to a substituted pyrrole ring system, preferably comprising a [3+2] cycloaddition. 15. Process according to claim 14, further comprising converting and R5 to hydrogens. Bisamide compound of the formula I Formula I or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt, ester, amide or stereoisomer thereof, wherein each of Rl to R6 is as defined as in any one of claims 1 to 11. 17. Bisamide compound obtainable by a method according to claim 15, being (3R,5R)-7-(N-(carboxy(4-fluorophenyl)methyl)isobutyramido)-3,5- dihydroxyheptanoic acid. 18. Use of a method according to any one of claims 1-15, or a bisamide compound according to claim 16 or 17, in the manufacture of atorvastatin or a derivative thereof.
